title: scleranthus annuus subsp. ruscinonensis
intro: Scleranthus annuus subsp. ruscinonensis, also known as Ruscinan knotgrass, is a subspecies of the annual knawel plant, belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and minor injuries. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and is also used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Scleranthus annuus subsp. ruscinonensis is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, which can be used internally or applied topically to the affected area.
